sections.text:
A.A. for
the Older
Alcoholic
—Never too late

A.A. for the
Older Alcoholic —
Never too late
A LARGE PRINT EDITION

ALCOHOLICS ANONYMOUS® is a fellowship of people
who share their experience, strength and hope with
each other that they may solve their common problem
and help others to recover from alcoholism.
The only requirement for membership is a desire
to stop drinking. There are no dues or fees for A.A.
membership; we are self-supporting through our own
contributions.
A.A. is not allied with any sect, denomination, politics,
organization or institution; does not wish to engage in
any controversy; neither endorses nor opposes any
causes.
Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other
alcoholics to achieve sobriety.
— Copyright © by AA Grapevine, Inc.;
reprinted with permission.
Revised large-print format copyright © 2023
by Alcoholics Anonymous World Services, Inc.
All rights reserved.
Mail address:
Box 459, Grand Central Station, New York, NY 10163
www.aa.org
17M – 7/24 (PAH)

A.A. for the
Older Alcoholic —
Never too late

CONTENTS

Was Getting Worse — Robert . . . . . . . . . 18

at 75 Years of Age — Barb . . . . . . . . . . . . 30

My First A.A. Meeting — Ann . . . . . . . . . . 45

Each Morning — Alfred . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 51

How do I find A.A.? . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 73

Alcoholics Anonymous . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 75
The Twelve Traditions of
Alcoholics Anonymous . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 77
